title: Mom-house-by-the-river
html:
Mom-house-by-the-river — the lifelong recurring aspiration that anchors every Yahir-as-builder note. "Buy my mom a relaxing house by a relaxing river." First written in June 2022; recurring through 2026.

The line, across the corpus

  • June 2022: "get mom house by the riverll" (typo in original) — first written instance.
  • Oct 2022: "my mom will remain in this vicious financial cycle if I don't do something to break her out."
  • Oct 2022: "Buy my mom a house" — listed as goal alongside "Have a beautiful connection with an amazing and smart woman."
  • Dec 2023: "buying my mom a relaxing house by a relaxing river — This one is clear, its absolute and we are going to do this. Lets accelerate it."
  • Oct 2024: "I will buy my my a relaxing life by a relaxing river" (typo: "my my" — the line is so internalized it autocompletes).
  • March 2025: counterpoint — mom still at Olive Garden, mom-house still unbought, "is building protein gonna fucking change anything I don't get my mom out of Olive Garden change?"

Why it matters

This is the root motivation for the founder grind / the Brotein crunch / the Austin move / the Rankett $100k/mo target. Yahir's commercial ambition is not abstract wealth-pursuit — it has a specific named beneficiary and a specific named outcome (a house, by a river, for his mom).

The dad-as-mirror anti-motivation

Pairs with the dad as mirror dread — buying mom a house is partly insurance against being his dad, who never escaped his cycle.

Open questions

  • Has Yahir bought mom anything substantial yet? (As of vault creation, no — she's still at Olive Garden.)
  • The river — Oregon? Texas? Mexico?
